Updated: 11/20/2000
Important news in blue



77 new episodes of DBZ airing Monday through Thursday at 5pm on Cartoon Network (began September 4th).



New DBZ on YTV (The episodes air when YTV receives them. The schedule is very fluid).



Viz will probably release future graphic novels uncensored. A press release is expected in a few weeks.



DBZ CCG Hummer Tour in full swing.



Movie #4, #5, and #6 slated for U.S. release in 2001.



FUNimation has licensed Infogrames/GT Interactive as a developer of DBZ games for PS, PS 2, and Gameboy Color. Expect nothing until 2002.


DVD (FUNimation):

The History of Trunks
(12/12/2000)

Bardock:
The Father of Goku
(Dec/Jan)

Android DVDs
(January)


VHS (FUNimation):

Imperfect Cell:
Discovery
(12/26/2000)
Race Against Time
(12/26/2000)
17's End
(2/20/2001)

Perfect Cell:
Hunt for 18
(2/20/2001)

Kai Tournament:
Opening Ceremony
(1/23/2000)
Final Round
(1/23/2000)

Great Saiyan Man:
Declaration
(3/20/2001)
Gohan's Secret
(3/20/2001)


Manga (Viz):

DB Part 3:
#7
(12/7/2000)
#8
(1/5/2001)
#9
(2/9/2001)
#10
(3/7/2001)

DBZ Part 4:
#1
(12/7/2000)
#2
(1/12/2001)
#3
(2/9/2001)
#4
(3/14/2001)

DB Graphic Novels:
Vol. 3
(12/7/2000)
Vol. 4
(2/9/2001)

DBZ Graphic Novels:
Vol. 3
(1/15/2001)
Vol. 4
(3/7/2001)

Which current FUNimation voice actor is your favorite?

Chris Sabat (Vegita, Piccolo)
Sean Schemmel (Goku, Kaio)
Sonny Strait (Kuririn, Bardock)
Linda Chambers (Furiza)
Tiffany Vollmer (Buruma)
Stephanie Nadolny (Gohan)
Dameon Clarke (Cell, Adult Gohan)
Eric Johnson (Trunks)
John Burgmeier (Tenshinhan)

Note: Our E-mail is currently down. It should be working by Friday. Thanks for your patience.
Current Features: Contest
Thursday - 11/30/2000

Mr.E @ 15:17 pst - Some Quotes Regarding the Upcoming Games...
Nothing in terms of info, just Infogrames and FUNi giving the official thumbs up...

``We are extremely pleased to partner with FUNimation to bring Dragon Ball Z to video game fans,'' said Paul Rinde, senior vice president and general manager of Infogrames' WizardWorks division. ``Dragon Ball Z is a favorite among millions of kids and is one of the most highly searched topics on Lycos. We're confident we can bring the characters into a new medium for its growing fan base to enjoy.''

``Infogrames was the clear choice for publishing the next series of Dragon Ball Z games,'' said Gen Fukunaga, president of FUNimation Productions, Ltd. ``Their expertise in taking well-known licenses and bringing them to life, while representing their true integrity of the storyline and characters, will surely please our Dragon Ball Z fans.'

I would expect to see some technical information after the holidays...until then, it'll be buzz. Courtesy of the Anime News Service.

Mr.E @ 15:14 pst - FUNimation Makes Pact with Satan...
Disney and Dragonball...a match made in heaven, or hell?

DBZ Merchandise will be given away on Radio Disney. Giveaways will begin in Mid December and will be given out everyday for 100 days. Radio Disney host a contest every hour, in which they give away prizes. One item of DBZ Merchandise will be given each day for 100 days. The Merchandise will vary, so be listening soon for your chance to win.

Well, it's free stuff...so I guess we have no right to complain. Just don't sign anything...especially if asked to do so in blood. From the official site...
Wednesday - 11/29/2000

Mr.E @ 23:59 pst - Bid For Power Candy...
BFP has posted three more screenshots showing off their delicious mod for Q3. Check em out on their main page.

Mr.E @ 23:57 pst - Palisades Joins the DB Merchandise Race...
With the Irwin news, this may not be the best time to enter the market...

Palisades Marketing has announced that they have signed a license to produce a series of resin statues and mini-busts based on the phenomenally popular manga and anime series created by Akira Toriyama.

The design work on these new DBZ products has just begun, and there isn't an official release date scheduled at this point. Late Spring 2001 is likely, but far from carved in stone. Dragon Ball Z fans are a demanding lot, and Palisades is committed to creating some of the coolest DBZ items on the market.

Resin statues and mini-busts...I haven't seen anything like that... ^_- Will be interesting to see what they come up with. Full article here.

Mr.E @ 23:53 pst - DBZ Magazine from Beckett...
Everyone's jumping on the band-wagon...

Dallas-based Beckett Publications has introduces a Dragon Ball Z collector's magazine. The monthly magazine Beckett Dragon Ball Z Collector first hit the newsstands on November 1 for $3.99.

UMJAMS again on this one...

Mr.E @ 23:44 pst - IRWIN PRESIDENT RESIGNS!
This could mean bad, bad things...

Reuters reports that George Irwin resigned on Monday as president and chief executive of Irwin Toy Ltd. as analysts speculated its sales were less than stellar heading into the crucial Christmas season. Irwin is best known for its Dragon Ball Z figures. Macdonald Irwin, company chairman and father of George Irwin, has taken over the president's role until a permanent replacement was found and a search committee has been struck to find a new president and chief executive, the company said. Maureen Atkinson, an analyst at J.C. Williams Group, said that as a manufacturer, Irwin Toy will have already had its ''kick at Christmas'' since retailers have stocked holiday merchandise by this point. George Irwin's departure likely came in the wake of disappointing holiday sales, she said. ``Obviously the results were not good..If it hadn't sold by now, it wasn't going to sell,'' she said. ``Probably the best thing that could happen to it would be if it were bought out.' 'George Irwin, who had been president of the 75-year-old Canadian company since 1990, faced a self-admitted ``major challenge'' to his leadership in October 1999 when board-level executives questioned his management following two years of losses at the company. Three Irwin executives -- George Irwin's brother, cousin, and uncle -- resigned at the time amid speculation of a family feud. The struggle resulted in what the company, which is majority-owned by the Irwin family, said was ``a new direction with new people.'' Analyst Richard Talbot of Talbot Consultants International said the timing of Irwin's resignations suggests trouble with the company's Christmas orders. ``It's a funny time to go. Normally if there's problems with toys, you usually wait until after the Christmas time,'' Talbot said. ``Obviously things are not well in Irwin land if people are being booted before Christmas.'' In the statement, MacDonald Irwin said the company was ''stronger today than at any point in (its) 75-year history.''

So what does this mean? Well, no one knows yet...but I personally doubt anything good will come of this. Just when Irwin started making quality toys, something like this has to happen. We wish Irwin the best of luck in hard times. UMJAMS spotted this story first, read the news on Yahoo here...
Tuesday - 11/28/2000

Mr.E @ 22:43 pst - Poll...
So you guys all want PS2's eh? *Strokes his brand new console* I can say I'm quite satisfied with this purchase, many people would love to get their hands on this one...

Dreamcast.

Anyhow, a new, more serious poll this go-around...which voice actor's performance speaks to you? Note that not all the voices are listed in the poll...a complete list can be found here.

Mr.E @ 22:07 pst - The Whip is Mightier than the Pen...
After weeks of Kaio complaining about "homework" (pfft...like his future is that important), I managed to beat a movie summary out of him. You'll find that a synopsis and review of movie #5 have been added to our movie guide. That leaves two DBZ movies, four DB movies, and a GT special to summarize (Yes, there is a GT special...I just know next to nothing about it). As a bonus, I've added four more biographies to the section of the same name. This batch is all from the original DB, as our DBZ bios are 95% complete.

Uh oh, looks like Kaio missed his math assignment...everyone stop by and wish him luck on his new burger-flipping enterprise (Which, as I am told, makes more than a job at FUNi).

Mr.E @ 00:24 pst - Episode Chart Update...
Page two is half up, covering episodes 100-150. Once again, thanks to Daimao for the Japanese titles...and special thanks to Dende who filled in a few English titles I was missing. Check it out here...
Monday - 11/27/2000

Mr.E @ 23:22 pst - Hmmm...
Well, I'm not going to apologize for the slow state of news recently...part of that has to do with our e-mail going to the land of wind and ghosts...and the rest is the fault of there being little to report. This is the big week where all my college applications are due; after this, I'm almost FREE! SkullMac is quite ill, and Kaio is...well, Kaio. The server is still not upgraded...and I was charged for it. I have a feeling we may not remain with these people much longer unless something changes this week. You can expect more info section updates in the near future. Now if you'll excuse me, THPS 2 beckons... :P

Update: Realized I was sitting on a small bios update: Tao Pie Pie. You can now find him in that section.

Kaio @ 18:07 pst - The ol' Korner is Back Again, Fellas!
With your weekly dose of idiocy comes a blinding flash of the ol' Kaio's Korner. It's all kinds of stupid, so you best pay it a visit, or else it's time to lay on the Mr. T style hurt.
Sunday - 11/26/2000

Mr.E @ 15:57 pst - Today's BFP Quickie...
The folks at Bid For Power have posted a small movie of their new Brolli model in action. The server may be a little slow, as it's been bombarded with traffic (Perhaps that's partly our fault ^_-).

Update: Six new screenshots have just been posted on their main page. Check 'em out.
Friday - 11/24/2000

Mr.E @ 09:57 pst - BFP Goodness...
Every time we see new stuff from this project, I'm going to make a post...even if they do it every day. Bid For Power has 13 brand new screenies of their growing variety of energy attacks. Shots like this one are just too damn cool...

Kaio @ 09:28 pst - "How Can a Fish Eat Laughs?"
Though the title has absolutely nothing to do with my update, I'll give you some extra "Kaio Points" (also known as "nothing") if you can tell me from where I picked that quote. Hell, your emails can't be worse than the one I just recieved telling me which stock buying guides are the most interesting, or the one which claimed that Mr. E had been kidnapped. Regardless of the mind-numbingly stupid pollution in my mailbox, however, I did update.

Surprisingly, there were actually NO new fanfic chapters. What's up with that, authors? Anyway, five new card strategies have been added to the card game section, and a couple new Freeza Saga pictures have been added to the same page. There are also some new chapters over at the fanmanga section. Well, I'll be leaving for a plane for Canada in a couple minutes, and while I'm there I may have time to type up some Kaio's Korner stuff. If I do, it will be posted when I return on Sunday evening, or possibly on Monday.
Thursday - 11/23/2000

Mr.E @ 11:12 pst - BFP Screenies!
More cranberry goodness care of Bid For Power this Thanksgiving. Not only have they moved to their new server, but they have 5 more screenshots for you to drool over. This series of images exhibits the melee aspect of the game, which really hasn't been shown off before. That's probably going to be the most difficult thing to implement in this game, as I can't see Q3 behaving like Tekken or Virtua Fighter. Anyhow, go have a look at those screenies! (Note: you may not be able to access their new server yet, not all ISPs have switched over...kind of like our current situation with @home, those bastards)

Mr.E @ 10:45 pst - DBZ Store Stuff...
Looks like the DBZ Store prepped themselves for the holidays, and have updated their look for the occaision. They are offering a couple more products that fit into the "mostly useless items with a Goku picture on them" category. The store has cut back the prices on the tapes...but not the DVDs. Honestly, I don't think FUNimation wants you to buy their quality products...

When Watson stuffs his face with turkey today, I hope he chokes...just a little bit. ^_^ Oh Barry, you know I love you...I wouldn't dream of insulting your fine work...*cough*

Previous News

Disclaimers
Planet Namek is just a fansite, we did NOT create Dragonball, nor did we have any part in the production process.

©2000, Bird Studio/Shueisha, Toei Animation. Licensed by FUNimation Productions, Inc. All Rights Reserved, or as otherwise directed by FUNimation. This site is not meant to infringe on any copyrights, it is simply here to encourage people to watch the Dragonball series.

Although we did not create the series, we did take a lot of time to work on this site and bring you original files and information. Please honor our efforts by not stealing our files/layout from us. Thank you.

Web Page Design © Planet Namek

Best viewed in 800 by 600 resolution or better & thousands of colors or more.
For the best experience, set your browser to view images "better" rather than "faster."
